From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1997 single by Foxy Brown

| "I'll Be" | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| from the album  *[Ill Na Na](/wiki/Ill_Na_Na "Ill Na Na")* | |
| [B-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side "A-side and B-side") | "La Familia" |
| Released | March 4, 1997 (1997-03-04) |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Hip hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") |
| Length | 3:00 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | * [Violator](/wiki/Violator_(company) "Violator (company)") * [Def Jam](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings") |
| [Songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter") | * [Shawn Carter](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") * [Jean-Claude Olivier](/wiki/Poke_(producer) "Poke (producer)") * [Samuel Barnes](/wiki/Samuel_Barnes_(Trackmasters) "Samuel Barnes (Trackmasters)") * [Angela Winbush](/wiki/Angela_Winbush "Angela Winbush") * [René Moore](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Moore "René Moore") * Bobby Watson * [Bruce Swedien](/wiki/Bruce_Swedien "Bruce Swedien") |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Trackmasters](/wiki/Trackmasters "Trackmasters") |

| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | "[Get Me Home](/wiki/Get_Me_Home_(song) "Get Me Home (song)")"  (1996) | "**I'll Be**"  (1997) | "[Big Bad Mamma](/wiki/Big_Bad_Mamma "Big Bad Mamma")"  (1997) | | |
|  | |
| [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") singles chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | "[Can't Knock the Hustle](/wiki/Can%27t_Knock_the_Hustle_(Jay-Z_song) "Can't Knock the Hustle (Jay-Z song)")" (1996) | "**I'll Be**" (1997) | "[Feelin' It](/wiki/Feelin%27_It_(Jay-Z_song) "Feelin' It (Jay-Z song)")" (1997) | | |
|  | |

"**I'll Be**" is a song recorded by American rapper [Foxy Brown](/wiki/Foxy_Brown_(rapper) "Foxy Brown (rapper)") for her debut studio album, *[Ill Na Na](/wiki/Ill_Na_Na "Ill Na Na")* (1996), featuring Brooklyn-based rapper [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"). It was released as the second single from the album on March 4, 1997, by [Violator](/wiki/Violator_(company) "Violator (company)") and [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings"). The song was written by [Shawn Carter](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Jean-Claude Olivier](/wiki/Poke_(producer) "Poke (producer)"), [Samuel Barnes](/wiki/Samuel_Barnes_(Trackmasters) "Samuel Barnes (Trackmasters)"), [Angela Winbush](/wiki/Angela_Winbush "Angela Winbush"), [René Moore](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Moore "René Moore"), Bobby Watson and [Bruce Swedien](/wiki/Bruce_Swedien "Bruce Swedien") with production by [Trackmasters](/wiki/Trackmasters "Trackmasters"), and samples [René & Angela](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_%26_Angela "René & Angela")'s 1985 song "I'll Be Good". It was recorded at [Chung King Studios](/wiki/Chung_King_Studios "Chung King Studios") in New York City, while the mixing of the track was finished at [The Hit Factory](/wiki/The_Hit_Factory "The Hit Factory"). "I'll Be" is a [hip hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") and [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") song with explicit lyrics that revolve around sex and money.

"I'll Be" received positive reviews from [music critics](/wiki/Music_journalism "Music journalism") and was a commercial success. In the United States, it peaked at number seven on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), becoming both Brown and Jay-Z's highest charting single at the time. On the [Hot Rap Singles](/wiki/Hot_Rap_Songs "Hot Rap Songs") chart, it reached number two and also peaked within the top 40 in other countries, such as Netherlands, New Zealand and the United Kingdom. "I'll Be" was certified [gold](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) two months after its release. It has sold over 500,000 copies and became one of the best-selling records of 1997.[[1]](#cite_note-billboardsales-1) To date it remains Brown's only solo top-40 single and her only single to earn a certification.

An accompanying [music video](/wiki/Music_video "Music video") was directed by [Brett Ratner](/wiki/Brett_Ratner "Brett Ratner"). "I'll Be" was nominated for Top Hot Rap Single at the 1997 [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award") and was ranked number 52 on [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1")'s "100 Greatest Hip-Hop Songs" list.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
